title: Add custom graph editor — let users create their own graphs for graph algorithms
description: QA GAP: Sorting has custom array input (AlgorithmPanel.tsx:1140) but graph algorithms use FIXED sample graphs only (SAMPLE_GRAPH_FOR_ALGORITHM in sample-graphs.ts). Users cannot add/remove nodes or edges. algorithm-visualizer.org and VisuAlgo both let users provide custom input for ALL algorithm types. Add: (1) 'Custom Graph' option alongside existing sample graphs, (2) Click canvas to add nodes, (3) Click+drag between nodes to add edges with weight input, (4) Delete nodes/edges, (5) Set start node. This is the #1 interactivity gap vs competitors.
acceptanceCriteria: Custom Graph option visible when a graph algorithm is selected
User can click to add nodes (auto-positioned or click-to-place)
User can drag between nodes to create edges with weight prompt
User can delete nodes/edges (right-click or delete key)
User can set start node for BFS/DFS/Dijkstra
Custom graph can be run through any graph algorithm
Minimum viable: 2-20 nodes supported

title: Add live code editor with run-and-visualize for user-written algorithms
description: QA GAP: algorithm-visualizer.org's #1 differentiator is that users can write JS/Python/C++ code and see it visualize step-by-step. Our ALG-226 adds display code (read-only) but users cannot EDIT or RUN custom code. Add: (1) Code editor panel (Monaco or CodeMirror) showing the algorithm in Python/JS, (2) User can modify the code, (3) Click 'Run My Code' executes in sandboxed Web Worker, (4) Step recording via instrumented array/graph proxy captures mutations, (5) Visualization plays back user's custom algorithm. This transforms from 'watch algorithms' to 'experiment with algorithms'. Frontend-only via Web Worker sandboxing.
acceptanceCriteria: Code editor panel togglable in the module layout
Pre-filled with selected algorithm's code in Python or JavaScript
User can edit the code
Run button executes code in sandboxed Web Worker with 5s timeout
Step recording captures array mutations as AnimationStep objects
Visualization plays back the user's modified algorithm
Infinite loop protection: 5-second timeout with clear error message

title: Add Python-Tutor-style execution trace showing variable states at each line
description: QA GAP: Python Tutor (pythontutor.com) shows line-by-line execution with variable states, stack frames, and heap visualization. Our ALG-179 (recursive call stack) and ALG-226 (Python display code) partially cover this but don't show VARIABLE STATE at each step. Add: alongside the code panel, show a 'Variables' panel that displays the current value of every local variable at the current algorithm step. For sorting: show array state, loop counters (i, j), comparison result, swap status. For graph: show visited set, distance map, queue contents. Synced with playback — stepping forward updates variables.
acceptanceCriteria: Variables panel visible alongside code panel when both are open
Shows current values of key variables at the current step
Sorting: shows array, i, j, swapped flag, pass number
Graph: shows visited set, distances map, queue/stack contents
DP: shows current cell coordinates, computed value, dependency values
Variables update in sync with step forward/backward
Expandable for complex objects (maps, arrays)

title: Add milestone bookmarks — jump to key algorithm moments (first partition, first merge, etc.)
description: QA GAP from professor scenario: a professor wants to show ONLY the Quick Sort partition step, not sit through 50 steps of comparisons to get there. Add: (1) Engine marks certain steps as 'milestone' (first pass complete, pivot placed, merge phase starts, etc.), (2) Timeline scrubber shows milestone markers as dots/ticks, (3) User can click any milestone to jump directly to that step, (4) Milestone labels shown on hover: 'Pass 1 complete', 'Pivot 7 placed at index 4'. This enables non-linear exploration — professors jump to the interesting part, students replay just the confusing section.
acceptanceCriteria: AnimationStep type has optional milestone?: string field
Top 10 algorithms have 3-5 milestones each (pass complete, pivot placed, merge start, etc.)
Timeline scrubber (or progress bar) shows milestone markers visually
Clicking a milestone jumps PlaybackController to that step
Milestone label shown on hover/tooltip
Keyboard shortcut: Shift+Right jumps to next milestone, Shift+Left to previous

title: Add print-friendly step export — generate PDF/printable study sheet of algorithm trace
description: QA GAP from student scenario: student wants to print the step-by-step trace as a study sheet for offline review or exam prep. Add: (1) 'Export Steps' button in toolbar, (2) Generates a clean, print-optimized HTML page with: algorithm name, input data, each step (number + description + array/graph state at that step), complexity summary, pseudocode, (3) CSS @media print styles for clean printing, (4) Optional: PDF download via browser print dialog. This enables: exam study sheets, professor handouts, offline review.
acceptanceCriteria: Export Steps button visible after running an algorithm
Generates printable HTML with all steps listed
Each step shows: step number, description, data state (array values or graph status)
Header shows: algorithm name, input data, total steps, complexity
Footer shows: pseudocode summary
CSS @media print removes non-essential UI elements
Opens in new tab ready for Ctrl+P / browser print
